The correct answer is H. The passage uses Pangloss’s absurdly optimistic philosophy, even after extreme suffering, to mock Leibnizian optimism and similar philosophical theories. Voltaire satirizes the idea that all events are for the best by placing Pangloss in ridiculous, horrific situations while he stubbornly clings to his beliefs. Thus, the "concatenation of universal events" highlights the satirical critique of philosophical systems through exaggerated, ironic scenarios.

\boxed{H}
